questionText: A cam follower moves along the path $x = t^{3} - 3t$ and $y = 3t^{2} + 1$, where distances are in millimetres and $-2 \le t \le 2$. Find the coordinates of the points where the follower is moving momentarily in the vertical direction (parallel to the $y$-axis).

steps.explanation: Motion parallel to the $y$-axis means there is no sideways movement, so we focus on the horizontal velocity $\dot{x}$.

steps.description: Set the horizontal velocity to zero
steps.explanation: The follower moves purely vertically at the instant its horizontal velocity vanishes, so we set $\dot{x} = 0$.
Both roots lie inside the allowed interval $-2 \le t \le 2$, so both are valid moments to check.

steps.description: Confirm the motion is genuinely vertical
steps.explanation: For truly vertical motion the vertical velocity must be non-zero (otherwise the point would be momentarily at rest). Here $\dot{y} \neq 0$ at both times, so both give vertical motion.
Substituting $t = 1$ into the horizontal equation gives the sideways position at that instant.
The same value of $t$ gives the height, so this is the first point of vertical motion.
Repeating for $t = -1$ locates the second point's horizontal position.
Substituting into the height equation gives the second point where the follower moves vertically.
Both points sit at the same height, on either side of the path, marking the two instants where the follower's motion is purely up-and-down.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The follower moves momentarily in the vertical direction at $(-2,\, 4)$ (when $t = 1$) and $(2,\, 4)$ (when $t = -1$).
